6.name: Annis Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- corrupted; see Society
6.environment: The Eternal Steppe (rocky foothills along the mountain borders, deep gullies, abandoned cave systems); secondary in Giant's Teeth foothills (Vestri territory border) and the Kinsweave (forested highland valleys)
6.organization: Solitary or covey (3 hags of any type)
6.description: She is nine feet tall and could be mistaken for a hill giant at a distance -- except that hill giants do not move like that, with the hunched, stalking patience of a thing that enjoys the chase more than the kill. Her skin is the blue-black of a deep bruise. Her fingers end in iron-hard nails the color of rust, and when she smiles -- and she will smile -- her teeth are flat, broad, and set in a jaw that could crack a horse's femur.

6.name: Ettin
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fragmented, faint; the ettin carries the residual earth-resonance of the Giant heritage, corrupted by degeneration into something dull and heavy
6.environment: Giant's Teeth (mountain caves, rocky overhangs, and abandoned Giant ruin-margins in the lower peaks; occasionally found in the Scorchspire foothills and the dry badlands between the Giant's Teeth and the Infinite Desert)
6.organization: Solitary, pair, gang (3-4), troupe (1-2 plus 1-2 brown bears), or band (3-5 plus 1-2 brown bears)
6.description: Two heads argue over which way to go. This is what you hear first -- the voices, deep and guttural, snapping at each other in a language that is almost Giant but not quite, the way a parrot's speech is almost language but not quite. Then the owner of the voices steps around the boulder and you see both heads on a body that is thirteen feet tall, hunched, broad as a cart, and carrying a morningstar in each hand. Both heads notice you simultaneously. They stop arguing. For the first time in minu...

6.name: Girallon
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the canopy and the trunk; girallons are creatures of the vertical forest
6.environment: Deepwood (the densest interior of the Great Forests -- specifically the mid-to-upper canopy zone, 60-120 feet above the forest floor, where the branches are thick enough to support their weight and the undergrowth is too dense for ground-travel)
6.description: Four arms. That is what you notice first -- not the size (though it is massive, nine feet at the shoulder), not the white fur (though it is matted and streaked with old blood), not the fangs (though they are longer than your fingers). Four arms, each one thick as a man's thigh, each one ending in a hand that could crush a helmet like an egg. The creature drops from the canopy on four of those arms and lands in front of you on two legs, and the remaining four spread wide, and every hand is open...

6.name: Jorogumo
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberration; breach resonance, deliberately masked
6.environment: Gatefall Reach (the waterfall-basins, pool-edges, and cliff-hollows of the Gatefall -- the great chain of waterfalls in Hasura territory where the river-systems descend from the highland forests to the lowland valleys; specifically the remote, infrequently-patrolled falls where prey can be taken without immediate discovery)
6.description: She is beautiful. She is always beautiful -- that is the point, that is the design, that is the trap. She sits at the edge of the Gatefall, where the forest canopy overhangs the waterfall-pool, and she combs her hair with her fingers and watches the water and waits for someone to find her. The Hasura scouts found the cocoons first -- three of them, wrapped in silk the colour of moonlight, hanging from the underside of the cliff-ledge behind the falls. Inside each cocoon, a Hasura elf. Two were ...

6.name: Maero (Brute)
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- artificial earth-attunement, cosmetic only
6.environment: Mountain Valleys (the densely-forested mountain valleys of Kovaka coastal territory -- the steep-walled river-valleys, old-growth ravines, and fog-bound coastal forests of the southwestern Great Forests)
6.organization: Solitary, pair, or band (3-5, rare -- degraded War-era formation)
6.description: The Kovaka call them te tangata mohoao -- the wild people -- but there is nothing of a person in what steps out of the tree-line. It is eight feet of matted hair and raw-boned muscle, its arms too long for its body, its hands thick-fingered and blunt as mallets. The face is wrong in the particular way that all Brute faces are wrong: close enough to Kin to trigger recognition, far enough from Kin to trigger revulsion. It smells of rotting bark and swamp-water and something chemical beneath both ...

6.name: Oni
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dark, primal; deep-forest earth, the resonance of soil that has been dark since the world began, where the forest-floor is older than the trees growing on it
6.environment: Deep Forests (Hasura territory and beyond -- the oldest, densest interior of the Great Forests where the canopy blocks all direct sunlight and the undergrowth is primordial)
6.description: The deep forest changes character past the Hasura border-markers. The trees are older, the canopy is lower, and the undergrowth is thick enough to hide a horse. The Hasura rangers who guided you this far have stopped. They will not go further. They point to a mark on a tree -- a claw-mark at nine feet, old and deep -- and speak the name quietly, as though the creature might hear: oni. In the forest beyond the marker, something laughs. It is a deep laugh, rich with amusement, and it comes from t...

6.name: Popobawa
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- shadow outsider; the popobawa exists in the space between elemental resonances, a predator of the gap, registering on Kin-sense as concentrated dread -- not absence (like Dómnathar void) but active malice, the feeling of being watched by something that feeds on fear
6.environment: Scorchspire Settlements (the stone-built villages, farming-terraces, and valley-floor communities of Taraf-Imro territory in the Scorchspire foothills -- specifically the isolated settlements at volcanic-valley edges where darkness is absolute at night and where the gap between houses creates shadows deep enough for a predator to hide)
6.organization: Solitary (always -- popobawa are aggressively solitary and territorial)
6.description: The settlement goes quiet after dark -- not the comfortable quiet of a village at rest, but the held-breath quiet of prey that knows the predator is already inside the pen. The Taraf-Imro warrior on the roof has not moved in twenty minutes. He is watching the shadows between the stone houses, watching for the thing that does not have a fixed shape, that moves through walls without opening doors, that smells of sulphur and bat-musk and something worse -- the sweet-rot stink of a creature that fe...

6.name: Tendriculos
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ravenous growth; the resonance of earth-fertility pushed to destructive excess, soil so rich and so hungry that everything planted in it grows beyond control and consumes everything around it
6.environment: Deepwood (the densest, darkest zones of the Great Forests -- the old-growth interiors where the canopy blocks nearly all light and the undergrowth is so thick that navigation is by sound and touch rather than sight)
6.description: The Deepwood is dense -- dense enough that sunlight reaches the forest floor only in thin, scattered beams, and the undergrowth is a tangle of moss, root, and fallen branch. The mound ahead looks like a compost-heap, green with new growth. It is not a compost-heap. The vines that compose it are moving, slowly, independently, and the opening at the mound's centre -- the one that looks like a hollow in the vegetation -- is lined with thorns the length of daggers, and it is a mouth.

6.name: Wyvern
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of Fire (Air/Fire) -- warm-hill predator; the resonance of warm updraughts rising from sun-heated hillsides, the air-fire intersection that produces the thermal columns wyverns ride to altitude -- not the intellectual fire of dragons or the sacred wind of thunderbirds, but the simple, functional heat that a predator uses to fly
6.environment: Warm Hills (the sun-heated hill-country and low ridgelines of central and southern Oreka -- specifically the foothills where thermal updraughts are reliable, where the terrain provides cliff-nesting sites and the plains below provide prey, and where the warm-season climate supports year-round flying)
6.description: The shape in the sky resolves as it banks -- two legs, two wings, a barbed tail longer than its body, and a head that is all jaw and no subtlety. It is not a dragon. It does not have a dragon's intelligence, a dragon's breath, or a dragon's treasure-hoard ambition. What it has is a tail-stinger loaded with enough venom to drop a horse, a wingspan that lets it carry that horse into the sky, and the single-minded predatory focus of a creature that has identified you as the right size for dinner.

6.name: Hobgoblin Captain (Fighter 5)
flags: hobgoblin
hostile
boss
special_qualities: Field Promotion: when the Captain takes a standard action to issue a command, all hobgoblins within 30 ft gain +1 to hit for 1 round
6.environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren)
6.description: A hobgoblin captain in articulated plate, wearing an iron lamellar sash of rank worked with the seven broken-chain marks of the Shattered Host.

7.name: Black Pudding
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- oozes exist outside the elemental framework; see Elemental Null
7.environment: Underground (Dómnathar tunnel networks, Giant ruin undercrofts, natural cavern systems beneath any region); most commonly encountered in the deep tunnels of the Kinsweave, Gatefall Reach, and beneath the Infinite Desert
7.description: The tunnel floor is wet. That is not unusual -- water seeps through stone in the deep places of the world, collecting in low points, forming pools that reflect lamplight. But this pool does not reflect. It absorbs. The light falls into it and does not come back. And the pool is moving, creeping uphill against the grade, flowing toward the warmth of your torch with a slow, blind hunger.

7.name: Chimera
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) dominant, with traces of Lord of Stone (Earth) and Youngest Brother (Air) -- a creature of three natures, none fully integrated
7.environment: Steppe-Mountain Border (the rocky highlands where the Eternal Steppe meets the Giant's Teeth foothills; secondary presence in the Scorchspire foothills and the dry ridgelines between the Kinsweave and the Infinite Desert)
7.description: Three heads. That is the first thing you register -- three heads on a body that should not be able to support even one of them. The lion's head roars. The goat's head screams. The dragon's head, dark-scaled and smouldering, simply opens its mouth and breathes fire across the rocks where you were standing a moment ago. The body is massive, a fusion of lion and goat with bat-wings spreading wide enough to blot out the pass behind it. It does not look like something that was designed. It looks li...

7.name: Dragonne
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity; the dragonne is a warm-stone predator, fire in a lion's body
7.environment: Warm Mountains and Foothills (Scorchspire foothills, southern Giant's Teeth, dry ridgelines between the Infinite Desert and the Eternal Steppe)
7.description: It crouches on the ridgeline like something that could not decide whether to be a lion or a dragon and chose both. Its body is a lion's -- massive, tawny, thick with muscle -- but its head is elongated, half-snouted, with brass-coloured scales running from brow to shoulders. Enormous bat-leather wings fold along its flanks, and when it opens its mouth to roar, the sound that comes out is not a lion's roar and not a dragon's bellow but something between: a brassy, resonant blast that shakes the ...

7.name: Dire Bear
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth (the mountain-and-forest bear, stone-dense and rooted)
7.environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills (forested mountain slopes, river valleys in the lower Giant's Teeth, the wooded approaches to Orean plateau settlements)

7.name: Goreham (Brute)
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- artificial earth-attunement, cosmetic only
7.environment: Temperate Hills (the low foothills and rolling terrain between the Giant's Teeth and the settled lowlands -- lower elevation than Hrokk territory, further from mountain peaks than Fogwrack lairs, occupying the most accessible terrain where Brutes can sustain themselves on livestock, game, and stolen food)
7.organization: Solitary, pair, gang (3-4), or mob (5-8, rare -- a degraded War-era formation, less cohesive than Hrokk warbands)
7.description: It is ten feet tall and most of that is gut. Where the Fogwrack is a mockery of Giant grace and the Hrokk is a mockery of Giant efficiency, the Goreham is a mockery of Giant endurance -- a lumbering, thick-skinned, dim-witted slab of Dómnathar-manufactured flesh that exists to absorb damage and keep moving forward. It is not fast. It is not clever. It is not frightening in the way the Fogwrack is frightening. It is simply, relentlessly, stupidly present, and it will not stop coming until it is...

7.name: Impundulu
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- lightning; the impundulu is the storm made predatory, the point where wind becomes fire
7.environment: Deep Steppe (the open grasslands of the Eternal Steppe's interior, far from mountains or forest -- flat terrain where storms build without obstruction and the impundulu can hunt unimpeded by canopy or cliff)
7.description: The storm comes from nowhere. The sky was clear, the wind was calm, and now the air tastes of copper and the hair on your arms stands straight. Lightning strikes the ground fifty feet away -- not from a cloud but from a bird. It is enormous, black-feathered, white-winged, riding the static charge it generates like a ship rides a wave. Its eyes are blue-white and crackling. When it screams, the sound is thunder.

7.name: Nymph
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords -- pristine; the unbroken elemental harmony of the world as it was meant to be, expressed through living beauty
7.environment: Pristine Groves (isolated pockets of elemental perfection scattered across Oreka -- old-growth hollows, mountain springs, forest-clearings, and lakeside meadows where the elemental balance has never been disrupted, usually far from Kin settlement and often concealed by natural terrain)
7.description: The grove is a place that should not exist. You are in the foothills of the Giant's Teeth, where the forest is thin and the soil is rocky and nothing grows taller than scrub-oak -- but here, in this hollow between two ridges, there is a grove of ancient trees surrounding a pool of water so clear it looks like a window into the earth. The air is different -- warmer, sweeter, thick with the scent of flowers that have no business blooming at this altitude. Everything here is more alive than it sho...

7.name: Spectre
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- extinguished; the resonance of fire that has gone out, heat that has been removed, the Lady of Fire's domain inverted into its opposite -- not cold (which is the absence of heat) but the memory of heat, the agonising awareness of warmth that no longer exists
7.environment: Ashen Hollows (the dead volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills -- places where volcanic activity ceased, where the Lady of Fire's presence once burned and now does not, leaving a residue of elemental grief that produces the most powerful incorporeal undead in the region)
7.description: The Ashen Hollows are the places where fire died -- the volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills where eruptions once poured lava and now pour nothing, where the ground is cold and grey and the trees are petrified stumps. Nothing lives here. That is the first sign that something dead does. The shape that rises from the grey earth is not smoke -- it is darker than smoke, more defined, and it moves with the deliberate purpose of something that remembers being alive and hates you for still be...

7.name: Tengu
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- canopy-wind; the resonance of wind in the highest forest, air channelled through branch-architecture into currents that only canopy-dwellers know, the Youngest Brother's domain at its most refined and most playful
7.environment: High Canopy (the upper canopy of the Great Forests -- specifically the Hasura canopy-settlement zones, where the forest-architecture of branch-platforms and vine-bridges creates an aerial environment that tengu navigate with natural ease)
7.description: The Hasura canopy-platform is a hundred feet above the forest floor, and the creature standing at the edge of the platform should not be able to stand there -- the branch is too narrow for anything larger than a squirrel. But the tengu stands with the casual balance of something born to the air, its taloned feet gripping the bark with an ease that makes the Hasura sentinel beside you look clumsy by comparison. It is man-shaped -- roughly -- with a raven's head, a raven's beak, and wings folded a...

7.name: Kobold Sorcerer-Chief (Sorcerer 7)
flags: kobold
hostile
caster
boss
7.environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; Gatefall major warrens
7.organization: warren-leader (with 1 kobold wizard, 1 dragon-priest, 2 trapsmiths, 12-20 lesser kobolds)
7.description: A kobold of truly unusual stature for her kind -- nearly four feet tall -- with a cape of overlapping metal scales scavenged from a dragon her grandmother killed. She has held the warren for thirty years and intends to hold it for thirty more.

8.name: Bodak
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- bodaks are breach-warped; they exist outside the elemental system entirely
8.environment: Gatefall Reach (Silence Breach zones, dead-Kin-sense areas near Dorrach Kael); secondary presence at the Lament of Kings (unstable resonance zones) and in deep Dómnathar tunnel networks where planar barriers are thin
8.description: It was alive once -- you can tell because it is shaped like something that was alive. Two arms, two legs, a head. But the flesh is the color of burnt tallow, stretched tight over a frame that seems to have been emptied from the inside. The eyes are the worst: pale, featureless white, like two holes punched through the face into a void behind it. It does not breathe. It does not blink. When it looks at you, something behind your sternum goes cold and stops.

8.name: Ogre Mage
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- cold; the sharp elemental signature of mountain-cold, ice on stone, the intersection where earth and water become something harder than either
8.environment: Cold Mountain Lairs (high passes, glacial caves, and mountain-top ruins in the upper Giant's Teeth and northern Scorchspires -- above the tree-line, in terrain too cold and remote for most settlements)
8.description: The cold comes first -- a drop in temperature sharp enough to see your breath, in a mountain pass where the air was merely cool a moment ago. Then the voice, speaking flawless Eruskan from somewhere above you, offers passage through the pass in exchange for a 'small consideration.' The speaker steps from behind a rock-face that should not have concealed anything that large: nine feet tall, blue-skinned, white-haired, wearing lacquered armour of a style you do not recognise, carrying a glaive a...

8.name: Ōmukade
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- toxic; heavy, metallic earth-resonance with a chemical-burn quality, like copper ore exposed to acid
8.environment: Deep Forest (Tanuki groves and the surrounding old-growth interior of the Great Forests -- the densest, darkest forest-floor zones where fallen trees create tunnel-like passages and the canopy blocks all sunlight)
8.description: The centipede fills the tunnel. That is the first and most important fact -- it fills the tunnel from wall to wall, from floor to ceiling, and it is moving toward you with a sound like a thousand knives being drawn across stone simultaneously. It is the colour of dried blood, its body is as thick as a wine-barrel, and every segment carries a pair of legs that end in hooks sharp enough to gouge stone. Its head is a nightmare of mandibles and compound eyes, and when it opens its jaws, the air fi...

8.name: Si'Lāt
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- false intimacy; a si'lāt generates water-resonance that mimics the personal, intimate quality of a qareen bond, creating a false sense of trust and familiarity that exploits the Na'wasua's deepest emotional connections
8.environment: Na'wasua Groves (the tidal grove-settlements, reef-caves, and lagoon-shelters of Na'wasua territory -- specifically the locations where Na'wasua community life is most intimate and where the boundary between welcomed guest and infiltrating predator is thinnest)
8.description: The Na'wasua grove is quiet -- the natural quiet of a reef-sheltered lagoon at low tide. The woman walking through the shallows ahead of you is beautiful in the way that Na'wasua sea-elves are beautiful -- long-limbed, dark-skinned, silver-haired, moving through the water with the unconscious grace of someone born to it. She turns. She smiles. And then your qareen -- the companion-spirit you have had since birth, the water-presence that has never left your side -- goes cold. Not distant. Cold. Th...

8.name: Stone Giant
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- Giant remnant; the resonance of the original Giants' earth-attunement, diminished and fragmented but recognisably derived from the same source -- the Lord of Stone's domain expressed through beings that carry the Giants' bloodline in diluted, feral form
8.environment: Giant's Teeth (the high mountain valleys, cliff-dwellings, and cave-systems of the Giant's Teeth range -- specifically the zones above Vestri mining-depth and below the snow-line, in the rock-country where stone giants can use their stone-throwing and rock-catching abilities to maximum tactical advantage)
8.organization: Solitary, gang (2-5), band (6-9 plus 35% noncombatants), or tribe (21-30 plus 50% noncombatants)
8.description: The boulder moves. Not falls -- moves, with the deliberate precision of something thrown by hands that know exactly where it will land. The second boulder follows three seconds later, and it lands exactly where the first would have driven you if you'd run. You look up. The figure on the cliff is sixty feet away and thirty feet tall, grey-skinned, long-armed, with a face like weathered granite and eyes that are calculating the trajectory of the third rock.

8.name: Treant
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ancient forest-foundation; the resonance of the earth beneath a forest's oldest roots, soil that has been forest-floor for so long that the trees and the earth have become one system -- not air (though the canopy moves in wind) but earth, the foundation from which all growth proceeds
8.environment: Great Forests (the old-growth hearts of the Great Forests -- specifically the zones where trees have grown undisturbed for centuries, where the root-networks are deep and interconnected, and where the forest's elemental identity is strongest)
8.description: The oak in the clearing is old -- older than the settlement downstream, older than the Pasua patrol-routes that pass its roots, old enough that its trunk is wider than three people standing shoulder to shoulder. You have walked past it twice today. You did not notice the face. You notice it now because the face is looking at you, and the eyes -- formed from knots in the bark, framed by ridges that were not there this morning -- express a patience so vast and so slow that your entire life is, to ...

8.name: Wendigo
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- frozen, corrupted; the resonance of wind stripped of warmth, the Youngest Brother's playful domain twisted into predatory cold -- not the natural cold of winter but the hungry cold of something that has consumed all warmth in its vicinity and still wants more
8.environment: Gatefall / Kinsweave Forests (the deep, cold forests of Gatefall Reach and the northern Kinsweave border-kingdoms -- specifically the zones where Dómnathar influence has thinned the elemental fabric and winter persists longer than it should, where the boundary between natural cold and supernatural hunger is thinnest)
8.description: The hunger comes before the creature does. You feel it first in your stomach -- a hollow, gnawing emptiness that has nothing to do with whether you've eaten -- and then in your mind, where it becomes something worse: the certainty that nothing will ever fill you, that the emptiness is permanent, and that the only thing that might -- might -- ease it is the warmth of another living body, broken open. The trees above creak. Something enormous moves through the canopy with a speed that should be imp...

8.name: Dark Dwarf Void-Smith (Expert 7 / Wizard 1)
flags: dwarf
dark_dwarf
hostile
caster
crafter
special_qualities: Void-forging: can craft items that suppress Kin-sense in a 5-ft radius
8.environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
8.description: A dwarf smith whose beard is threaded with silver wire and whose leather apron is embroidered with runes in a script not of this world. She makes the suppressor-irons that hide the Silentborn from Kin-sense.

8.name: Dómnathar Infiltrator (Rogue 6 / Fighter 2)
flags: domnathar
hostile
kin_silent
boss
special_qualities: Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
8.environment: Gatefall Reach (Silence Breach approaches, Fortress of Dorrach-Vel command); Deepwater Spur Tower outer
8.description: A tall, slender figure with skin the color of wet slate and eyes that catch the torchlight at an odd angle. His ears are so pointed they look sculpted. He has been killing Kin officers since the War and has not aged a day.

2.name: Bugbear
flags: elemental_affinity:None native -- Goblinoid (extraplanar origin, arrived with the Dómnathar host); free bugbears in contact with Oreka's elemental environment for centuries show trace Earth attunement
2.alignment: Usually chaotic evil (Deceiver-aligned) or usually neutral (free)
2.environment: Temperate mountains and forested highlands (Giant's Teeth foothills, Gatefall Reach ridgelines, Kinsweave hill-country); Deceiver-aligned bugbears are found in Dómnathar tunnel networks and underground strongholds
2.organization: Solitary, gang (2-4), or warband (5-12 plus 1-3 hobgoblin officers [Deceiver-aligned]) or hunting lodge (5-12 plus 1 elder [free])
2.description: It is bigger than you expected -- half again the height of a man, broad-shouldered, covered in coarse dark fur that smells of wet leather and something else, something sharp and wrong that prickles at the back of your Kin-sense. It moves through the forest with a silence that should be impossible for something its size, and when it looks at you from the underbrush, its yellow eyes hold an intelligence that is not malicious -- just utterly, carefully calculating whether you are worth the effort.

2.name: Hippogriff
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- similar to griffons but lighter, more air-dominant; the hippogriff is wind-over-hill rather than wind-over-mountain
2.environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills (the low, grassy hills and upland meadows of the Giant's Teeth margins -- lower elevation than griffon territory, in terrain where grassland meets slope and thermal-updrafts are strong)
2.description: The horse has wings. That is the simplest, most reductive description, and it is wrong -- the creature is not a horse with wings any more than a griffon is an eagle with a lion's body. It is its own thing: part raptor, part equine, shaped by the same elemental forces that produced griffons but with a different emphasis. Where the griffon is all predator, the hippogriff is half-wild, half-willing, and that willingness is what makes it valuable.

2.name: Satyr
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Youngest Brother (Earth/Air) -- forest-edge; the resonance of where cultivated land meets wildwood, earth-under-leaves meeting wind-through-branches, the boundary between the tamed and the untamed expressed as music
2.environment: Great Forests (the mid-forest zones between deep canopy and forest-edge -- specifically the clearings, stream-banks, and meadow-openings where sunlight reaches the ground and the forest is at its most welcoming and most deceptive)
2.description: The music reaches you before the musician does. It is coming from deep in the forest -- a pipe-melody that winds between the trees like smoke, changing key with each turn of the path, and you find yourself walking toward it without having decided to. The undergrowth parts. The clearing opens. He is sitting on a fallen oak, legs crossed -- the legs of a goat, furred and hooved, below the bare-chested torso of a man who looks like he has never done anything more strenuous than smile. He is playin...

2.name: Worg
flags: elemental_affinity:Wild Static -- worgs are intelligent non-Kin creatures that register on Kin-sense as unreadable presence, the same wild-static signature shared by all non-Kin sapients (goblins, goblinoids, Wargs); recognisable, alive, but not elementally identifiable
2.environment: Any (worgs range across all of Oreka's temperate and cold regions -- Eternal Steppe, Great Forest edges, mountain foothills, and the margins of every settled area)
2.description: The wolf is too large, too intelligent, and looking at you with an expression that wolves do not have. Wolves assess threats. This creature is assessing you -- your armour, your weapons, the way you hold your sword. It understands equipment. It understands threat-hierarchy. And when it speaks -- growling, accented Common -- it says: 'Move. Or I move you.'

2.name: Kobold Cultist (Cleric 2)
flags: kobold
hostile
caster
special_qualities: Spells/day: 0th -- 4 (cure minor, detect magic); 1st -- 3 (bane, shield of faith, inflict light wounds)
2.environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; also Gatefall and Deepwater
2.description: A stooped kobold in scorched temple-robes, a small silver ampoule of Dómnathar Void-Oil hung at her neck. She tends the warren's sick and the warren's dead with equal patience.

9.name: Amaru
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) primary, Lady of the Sea (Water) secondary -- the amaru is a creature of deep stone and mountain water
9.environment: Giant's Teeth (high passes, mountain valleys, cliff-face lairs above the treeline); secondary presence along the Orean plateau and in the volcanic foothills where the Giant's Teeth meet the Scorchspires
9.description: The mountain pass narrows to a throat of dark stone, and there -- coiled across the gap like a living gate -- lies something that is neither serpent nor dragon but partakes of both. Its body is scaled in copper and verdigris, the colour shifting with the angle of light like tarnished metal; its head bears two short, curving horns above eyes that glow with a deep amber intelligence. Wings -- not bat-leather but feathered, iridescent green-black like a condor's -- lie folded against flanks ribbed w...

9.name: Cherufe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity, Fire dominant; the cherufe is molten earth, the place where the Lady of Fire's domain and the Lord of Stone's domain merge
9.environment: Scorchspires (active calderas, magma chambers, volcanic vent systems, and the deep volcanic core of the Scorchspire mountain range)
9.description: The caldera glows. Not from below -- from across, from the thing that sits in the centre of the lava lake like a man sitting in a bath. It is made of stone. It is on fire. The stone is not burning -- the fire is coming from inside, pouring through cracks in a body of black basalt that has the approximate shape of a giant, if a giant were designed by someone who thought 'humanoid' was a loose suggestion rather than a requirement. Its eyes are magma. When it moves, the caldera trembles.

9.name: Inkanyamba
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- storm-water; the inkanyamba commands the intersection of water and wind, generating weather through elemental will
9.environment: Inland Lakes (the large, deep freshwater lakes of the Taraf-Imro territories in the southern mountains and volcanic lowlands -- specifically the caldera-lakes and tectonic rift-lakes that are deep enough to support a Huge aquatic predator year-round)
9.description: The lake has been wrong for three days. The fishermen noticed first -- the water-level rising when it should be falling, the currents running backward, the fish moving to the shallows in panicked schools. Then the storms started. Not regular storms -- targeted storms, centred on the lake, rotating with a focus that suggests intelligence rather than weather. Something beneath the surface is angry, and whatever it is, it is large enough to control the water above it.

9.name: Níðhöggr-Kin
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- inverted; the anti-resonance of something that consumes earth rather than expressing it, entropy given draconic form
9.environment: Giant's Teeth Roots (the deepest subterranean levels beneath the Giant's Teeth -- below Vestri mining depth, below lindworm territory, in the root-halls where petrified ancient forest meets living stone)
9.description: The root-hall goes deeper than the dwarves mapped. Below the Vestri deep-shafts, below the lindworm tunnels, below everything that lives in the dark by choice, there is a place where the mountain's roots meet the world's roots -- where stone becomes something older, something organic, something that grew rather than formed. The rock here is threaded with veins of petrified wood the size of aqueducts, remnants of trees that were old when the Giants were young. And something is eating them. You ...

9.name: Roc
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- sovereign; the most massive expression of air-resonance in Oreka's living creatures, wind given the weight and authority of a mountain, the sky's own apex predator
9.environment: Mountain Peaks (the highest peaks of the Giant's Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Ashen Crown -- above the snow-line, above the cloud-line, in the thin air where nothing else flies and the wind is the only law)
9.description: The shadow passes over the caravan and everything goes dark -- not like a cloud, because clouds don't move that fast and don't have that shape. You look up. The wingspan blots out the sun. It is a bird -- your brain insists it is a bird, because it has wings and feathers and a beak -- but it is a bird the way a mountain is a hill. It is the size of a ship. Its talons, each one longer than a man is tall, are open and reaching, and the thing it is reaching for is the lead pack-elephant, which weig...

9.name: Half-Dómnathar Battle-Priest (Cleric 7)
flags: half_domnathar
hostile
caster
boss
9.environment: Deepwater Marches (Spur Tower command levels); Gatefall Fortress of Dorrach-Vel
9.description: A Half-Dómnathar in blackened full plate, the chapter-priest of the Spur Tower's war-temple. His prayers call on an absence rather than a presence.

3.name: Cadi Kuşu
flags: elemental_affinity:Corrupted -- the Cadı Kuşu retains traces of its original Kin affinity, but the signature is cracked and wrong, contaminated by stolen Deceiver magic
3.environment: Gatefall Reach (settlements near the Silence Breach perimeter, abandoned farmsteads, forest edges); secondary presence in the Kinsweave border-kingdoms and Tidebloom Reach where Dómnathar influence reaches furthest
3.description: The bird perches on the rooftop at dusk -- dark-feathered, crow-sized, with eyes that catch the light wrong. It watches the village with a patience that no animal possesses. When it opens its beak, the sound that emerges is not a birdcall but something like a woman humming through a mouthful of glass. The villagers bar their doors. They know what a Cadı Kuşu is. They know what it used to be.

3.name: Derro
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- derro are alien to Oreka's elemental system; see Breach Resonance
3.environment: Deep Underground (natural cavern systems beneath any region, particularly the deep strata beneath the Kinsweave and Giant's Teeth where derro warrens intersect Vestri mining tunnels)
3.organization: Team (2-4), squad (5-8 plus 1 3rd-level sorcerer), or band (11-20 plus 30% noncombatants plus 3 3rd-level sorcerers plus 1 5th-level savant leader)
3.description: Small. Blue-skinned. Mad. Those are the three things every Vestri miner knows about derro, and they are listed in order of importance. Small enough to fit through cracks you wouldn't think twice about. Blue-skinned, the colour of deep-water bruise, which means you can lose them in torchlight shadow. Mad, which means they will do things no sane creature would do -- attack a party of armed dwarves with stone knives, set traps in tunnels they themselves use, experiment on captured prisoners with ...

3.name: Dire Ape
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth/Air (canopy and trunk -- the Deepwood Paragon Ape is an expression of forest verticality)
3.environment: Deepwood Canopy (the highest, densest sections of the Great Forests, particularly the southern Deepwood where the canopy forms an unbroken ceiling 100+ feet above the forest floor)
3.organization: Solitary or company (2-4, siblings from the same Paragon siring)

3.name: Ember Drake
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- pure, undiluted fire-affinity; the ember drake is the Lady's domain in its most accessible form
3.environment: Scorchspires (volcanic vents, lava tubes, caldera edges, and Rarozhki forge-halls); secondary presence in the Emberfang Mountains, Ashen Crown, and volcanic vents in the Infinite Desert
3.description: It sits on the forge-rim like a cat sits on a warm hearth -- compact, content, and radiating heat that makes the air shimmer. It is the size of a large dog, scaled in overlapping plates of orange-red that shift to white-hot at the edges, and its eyes glow like coals. A thin trail of smoke curls from each nostril. The Rarozhki smith working at the forge does not look up. The drake has been here longer than he has. It was here when his grandfather lit this forge for the first time, and it will b...

3.name: Grick
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberration; breach resonance indicates origin outside Oreka's elemental framework
3.environment: Underground (natural cave systems, mine-tunnels, and Giant-era passages -- anywhere with consistent darkness, moisture, and sufficient prey)
3.description: The tunnel narrows. The walls are wet. Something moves in the darkness ahead -- not quickly, not dramatically, but with the patient, questing motion of a creature that has all the time in the world and knows that everything in the tunnel eventually comes to it. You see tentacles first -- four of them, barbed, extending from the darkness like fingers testing the air. Then the beak.

3.name: Noppera-Bō
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberrant; a creature that registers on Kin-sense as a blank space in the shape of a person, like a silhouette cut from the world
3.environment: Great Forests (Tanuki groves and the surrounding old-growth forest -- particularly the twilight-zones between Tanuki settlements and the deeper forest, where the lantern-light ends and the shadows begin)
3.description: The path through the Tanuki grove is well-lit -- paper lanterns strung between the trees, their warm light turning the forest-floor into a patchwork of gold and shadow. You have been walking for twenty minutes and you are beginning to suspect that the path is longer than it should be. The figure ahead of you on the path is a Tanuki woman -- round-faced, small, carrying a basket of mushrooms. She has been walking ahead of you since you entered the grove. She has not turned around. You call out. ...

3.name: Pegasus
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- pure; the cleanest, most joyful air-signature in Oreka, flight as an expression of freedom
3.environment: Eternal Steppe (the open grasslands -- particularly the high-steppe regions where the horizon is unbroken and the wind runs unimpeded, far from the forest-edge and far from mountains, in the sky above the steppe's heart)
3.description: The herd moves across the steppe-sky at dawn -- a dozen winged horses silhouetted against the pink-gold horizon, their flight-formation as precise as any Mytroan cavalry column but achieved without riders, without commands, without anything except the collective grace of creatures for whom the sky is home. They are white, or silver, or pale gold, and they fly with a wingspan that should not support a horse-body but does, because the wind beneath them is not entirely wind. Something else holds ...

3.name: Rust Monster
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- corroded; the resonance of oxidation, of metal returning to ore, of the earth reclaiming what was taken from it -- entropy given form and appetite
3.environment: Vestri Mining Tunnels (the active and abandoned mine-shafts, ore-processing tunnels, and metal-storage facilities of the Vestri Dwarven mining complex in the Giant's Teeth -- specifically wherever metal is mined, stored, transported, or worked)
3.description: The thing is about the size of a large dog, with a body like an armadillo's, antennae that wave independently of each other like a dowser's rods, and a long, flat tail that it uses for balance as it scurries toward you with a speed that is frankly alarming for a creature of its build. But you are not alarmed by the creature itself. You are alarmed by the expression on the Vestri miner's face -- the miner who has faced olgoi-khorkhoi and cave-spiders and once punched a grick -- because the miner...

3.name: Svartálfar Shade
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fading; the resonance of Vestri earth-attunement diminished by death but not extinguished -- a candle's afterglow where a forge-fire once burned, recognisably dwarven, recognisably diminished
3.environment: Abandoned Dwarf Halls (the sealed, evacuated, or forgotten mining-halls and forge-complexes of the Vestri Dwarven infrastructure -- specifically the halls abandoned during the centuries of contraction following the Fall of Aldenheim, where Vestri dead were left in memorial-chambers when the living withdrew)
3.description: The dwarf-hall is empty. It has been empty for a long time -- the forge-fires are cold, the tool-racks bare, the sleeping-quarters stripped of everything portable. The Vestri who abandoned this hall took everything they could carry and left everything they could not, and what they could not carry included their dead. The shade drifts through the doorway of the memorial-chamber with the slow, deliberate movement of something that is still performing a duty it was assigned a very long time ago. ...

3.name: Unicorn
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- forest-heart; the resonance of the deepest, purest forest-earth -- soil that has never been ploughed, turned, or contaminated, the Lord of Stone's domain expressed as the foundation of untouched wilderness
3.environment: Great Forests (the deepest, most pristine zones of the Great Forests -- specifically the old-growth interiors where Kin settlements have never reached, where the forest-floor has been undisturbed for millennia, and where the elemental purity of the earth is absolute)
3.description: The forest clearing is lit from within -- not by sunlight (the canopy is too thick) but by something else, a quality of light that has no visible source and no shadow. The creature standing in the clearing is a horse -- or it looks like a horse, the way a cathedral looks like a building. It is white, impossibly white, the white of fresh snow before anyone has walked on it, and its mane falls like water. The horn is the colour of old ivory, spiralling from the centre of its forehead with the mat...

3.name: Kobold Sorcerer (Sorcerer 3)
flags: kobold
hostile
caster
special_qualities: Spells known include: magic missile, mage armor, burning hands, scorching ray, invisibility
3.environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren)
3.description: A kobold whose scales have faded to ash-grey from long hours near ritual-flame. His eyes are bright yellow and his claws are stained with different colors of dried reagent.

3.name: Kobold Trapsmith (Rogue 3)
flags: kobold
hostile
rogue
3.environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren); also Kinsweave ruins
3.description: A wiry kobold with a brass belt of loops full of iron teeth, caltrops, dart-needles, and glass phials. He smiles with very small very even teeth.

3.name: Hobgoblin Sergeant (Fighter 2)
flags: hobgoblin
hostile
special_qualities: Discipline: adjacent hobgoblins gain +1 morale to attack and save vs fear
3.environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren); Burnt Hollows
3.description: A hobgoblin in well-kept Dómnathar-issue chain and red tabard. He salutes crisply, speaks rarely, and breaks men who speak out of turn.

3.name: Flamewarg Cult Initiate (Sorcerer 3, Human)
flags: human
hostile
caster
cultist
special_attacks: Ritual self-immolation (healing others at cost of caster)
special_qualities: Cult marks visible on the forearms -- crossed-out suns in burn-scars
3.environment: Eternal Steppe (Burnt Hollows collapsed burrow-forts, Dark Dawn Battlefield dead zones); underground; Gatefall Scar Road; occasional Infinite Desert oasis raid camps
3.organization: cell (2-4 cultists plus 1 flamewarg and 2 goblins)
3.description: A lean human woman with charred sleeves and a half-healed burn on her cheek in the shape of a hand. She believes the Deceivers will return, and she will be rewarded.

5.name: Ccoa
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity; the ccoa is where the storm meets the mountain
5.environment: Giant's Teeth (high mountain ridgelines, storm-swept passes, exposed plateaus above the treeline); secondary presence along the Scorchspires' northern ridges where storms gather
5.description: The storm comes down the mountain wrong. It moves against the wind, coiling between the peaks like something alive. Lightning strikes the same ridgeline three times, four times, and in the flash you see it -- a shape in the clouds, sleek and low-slung, with the silhouette of an enormous cat. Its eyes are lightning. Its fur is thunder. It walks across the sky as though the storm were solid ground beneath its feet.

5.name: Cloaker
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- cloakers are alien aberrations; see Breach Resonance
5.environment: Underground (deep cavern systems, Dómnathar tunnel networks, Giant ruin undercrofts); most commonly encountered beneath the Kinsweave and the Infinite Desert, where deep tunnels connect natural caverns to ancient infrastructure
5.description: The ceiling is low here, the stone damp, and the shadows beyond the torchlight are thick as wool. You almost walk under it -- would have walked under it -- except your companion's torch catches a ripple of movement overhead and something that looked like a hanging sheet of dark leather opens two red eyes and a mouth full of needle-teeth and drops.

5.name: Kitsune
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- mutable; the kitsune's air-resonance shifts and changes like the wind, never quite the same twice, reflecting the fey's shapeshifting nature
5.environment: Great Forests (the deciduous and mixed forests of Hasura territory -- forest-edges, shrine-clearings, ancient game-trails, and the borderlands where managed forest meets wild wood)
5.organization: Solitary or skulk (2-4, always related -- a parent and offspring, or siblings)
5.description: The fox was there a moment ago. You saw it -- a red fox, large, sitting at the tree-line with its tail curled around its feet, watching you with an intelligence that foxes should not have. You blinked. Now it is a woman -- or was a woman, or is becoming a woman, or is something in between that your brain is trying to resolve into one shape or the other and failing. She smiles at you with the fox's eyes in a woman's face, and you understand that the shape was never the point.

5.name: Manananggal
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- undead, Silence Breach-origin
5.environment: Silence Breach Zones (the Kin-sense dead zones near Dómnathar surface-breach points -- particularly in the Gatefall Reach and the outer Kinsweave, where the residual metaphysical damage of the Deceivers' War entry-point creates conditions that sustain this specific form of undead)
5.description: You find the lower half first. It is standing upright in a Dómnathar-ruined basement, legs together, trunk severed cleanly at the waist, and it is warm. Blood pools slowly at the separation point but does not spill -- as though the body is waiting for something to return. The cellar smells of vinegar and old blood and the particular sterile absence of a place where Kin-sense dies. You hear wings above you -- heavy, wet, membranous wings -- and something laughs in the dark with a voice that used ...

5.name: Ochre Jelly
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- oozes exist entirely outside the elemental framework; they register on Kin-sense as a gap, a place where resonance should be but isn't
5.environment: Underground (Giant-built tunnel-complexes, Vestri deep-mines, natural cave-systems, Dómnathar tunnel-fringes -- any enclosed underground space with organic matter to consume)
5.description: The tunnel floor is wet. Not with water -- with something that has the colour of old amber and the consistency of warm tar, and it is moving. You notice it because the rat you were following has stopped running. The rat is dissolving. The amber substance has risen over the rat's legs, is climbing its body, and the rat is thrashing but cannot pull free. By the time the rat stops thrashing, the amber has consumed everything except the bones, which it leaves behind, clean and polished, on the tun...

5.name: Phase Spider
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- between; phase spiders exist partially outside the elemental framework, their resonance flickering between present and absent as they shift between planes
5.environment: Gatefall Reach Forests (the mixed deciduous forests of the Gatefall Reach -- particularly the border-zone near the Silence Breach where the planar membrane is thinnest and phase-shifting is easiest)
5.description: The web is invisible. Not hidden -- invisible. The spider spins its web on the Ethereal Plane and waits there, watching the Material Plane through the membrane between worlds, until a creature walks into the space that corresponds to the web's location. Then it shifts -- phases -- from the Ethereal to the Material, materialising directly behind its prey with fangs already extended. The attack lasts less than a second. Then the spider phases back, taking its venom and its victim's screams with it...

5.name: Pishtaco
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dead cold; mountain-earth drained of warmth, the resonance of stone that should be alive with geological heat but has been hollowed out, leaving only cold mass
5.environment: Mountain Passes (the high passes, narrow defiles, and single-track trails of the Giant's Teeth and surrounding ranges -- specifically the remote, infrequently-travelled passes between Orean foothill communities where travellers are isolated and rescue is days away)
5.description: The mountain pass smells wrong. You have been climbing for six hours through good clean air -- pine-resin, cold stone, the metallic scent of altitude -- and now something has changed. It smells like a butcher's shop on a warm day: blood and fat and the sweetish tang of meat beginning to turn. The Orean guide has stopped walking. He is looking at the path ahead, where the rocks narrow to a single-file passage between two cliff-faces, and he is looking at the ground, where something glistens in t...

5.name: Spider Eater
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- canopy-predator; the resonance of the Great Forest's upper airspace, wind filtered through leaves and channelled between tree-trunks
5.environment: Great Forests (the mid-canopy and upper-canopy zones of the Great Forests -- specifically the zones where giant spider populations are dense enough to support spider eater breeding cycles)
5.description: The thing drops from the canopy -- thirty feet of membranous wing spanning between a body like a wasp's, a head like a horse's, and a stinger the length of your forearm. It is not falling. It is diving, and the target is not you but the giant spider in the web between the oaks. The stinger drives into the spider's abdomen with surgical precision, deposits something, and the creature is airborne again before the spider can react. In three days, the spider's body will burst open. The larva insid...

5.name: Troll
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- feral vitality; the resonance of earth-as-regeneration, the Lord of Stone's domain expressed as the relentless, unkillable persistence of stone -- break it and it reforms, chip it and it grows back, erode it and it replaces itself
5.environment: Cold Mountains / Marshes (the northern Giant's Teeth foothills, the cold marshes bordering the Eternal Steppe's northern reaches, and the fog-wrapped mountain-valleys where trolls establish lairs -- anywhere cold, wet, and remote enough to support a large predator that eats constantly)
5.description: The thing comes out of the marsh at a lope -- six limbs of disproportionate length, a body like a collection of angles that someone forgot to soften, and a face that is all mouth. It is green, in the way that rot is green, and it smells like a compost-heap in summer, and it is very fast for something its size. But the thing about the troll -- the thing that every survivor mentions first -- is not the speed or the claws or the jaw. It is the arm you cut off that grows back. It is the wound that c...

5.name: Winter Wolf
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of the Sea (Air/Water) -- winter predator; the resonance of cold wind and frozen water combined, the two elements meeting in the winter-state that produces ice, frost, and the killing cold of the northern steppe
5.environment: Northern Steppe (the cold grasslands and snow-covered plains of the Eternal Steppe's northern reaches -- the border-zone between the steppe and the northern mountain-barrier where winters are long and wolves are large)
5.description: The wolf on the ridge is white -- white as the snow-field it stands on, invisible except for the eyes (amber, steady) and the breath (which is not breath but a thin, constant stream of ice-fog that curls from its jaws like smoke from a forge). It is larger than any wolf should be -- shoulder-height to a tall man -- and it watches you with an intelligence that wolves do not possess. When it speaks -- and it will speak, because winter wolves speak -- the voice is a low, grinding rasp, and the words ...

5.name: Yuki-Onna
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- a yuki-onna's resonance is frozen grief, undeath shaped by cold, the elemental connections of the original person iced over and preserved but inaccessible, like a voice heard through a frozen windowpane
5.alignment: Usually neutral evil (but see below -- some yuki-onna retain fragments of their living personality and may spare travellers under specific conditions)
5.environment: Northern Forests (Hasura) -- the snow-deep forests and mountain-passes of the Hasura homelands, where winter is long and the dead do not always stay buried beneath the snow -- specifically the high passes, blizzard-routes, and deep-winter trails where travellers are most vulnerable to exposure
5.description: The snow falls harder after midnight, and the trail disappears. You are not lost -- you know exactly where you are -- but the cold has reached the place inside your chest where decisions live, and you cannot quite remember why you were walking. You should sit down. You should rest. The snow is soft. The cold is not painful anymore; it is becoming warm, which you know -- in the part of your mind that is still thinking -- means you are dying. And then she is there: standing in the snow without foot...

5.name: Kobold Dragon-Priest (Cleric 5)
flags: kobold
hostile
caster
boss
5.environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
5.organization: solitary or leader (with 6-10 kobolds and 1 trapsmith)
5.description: An old kobold priestess, scale-crest gone white, who serves the red dragon her clan believes to sleep beneath Pryee. Her robes smell of kerosene and incense.

1.name: Darkmantle
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- faint, ambient; the darkmantle is a cave creature through and through
1.environment: Underground (natural cavern systems, Giant ruin undercrofts, Dómnathar tunnel fringes); found beneath any region with cave networks, most commonly reported in Vestri mining territories and the tunnels beneath the Infinite Desert
1.description: The stalactite opens its eyes. That is what it looks like -- a cone of wet stone hanging from the cavern ceiling suddenly sprouts two tiny, reflective eyes and unfurls a membrane of leathery skin that was folded so tightly against its body that it was invisible. Then it drops.

1.name: Grig
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the grig is the playful overlap of earth and air; the cricket in the grass, the music on the breeze
1.environment: Great Forests (forest clearings, meadow-margins, stream-banks, and the sun-dappled edges of the canopy -- wherever the forest is light and warm enough for music)
1.description: You hear the fiddle first. Something is playing a fiddle in the undergrowth -- playing it well, playing it fast, playing a reel that makes your feet want to move before your brain can object. You part the ferns and see the musician: a creature the size of your hand, cricket-legged, fairy-winged, grinning like a lunatic, sawing away at a fiddle the size of a matchstick with a bow the size of a needle. It sees you seeing it and plays faster.

1.name: Grimlock
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- grimlocks exist outside the elemental framework, like all non-Kin peoples
1.environment: Deep Underground (natural cave systems below the depth of Vestri mining, below Dómnathar corridors, in the deep strata where light has never reached and the concept of 'surface' is an abstraction)
1.organization: Gang (2-4), pack (10-20), or tribe (20-40 plus 1-3 leaders of 3rd-5th level plus 2-5 dire bats)
1.description: It has no eyes. That is the first thing you notice -- the flat, smooth expanse of face where eyes should be, unbroken by sockets, lids, or any concession to sight. It does not need eyes. It navigates by sound, by scent, by the vibration of your footsteps through the stone, and by a sense you cannot name that tells it exactly where you are, how large you are, and where to put the axe.

1.name: Monstrous Spider
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- faint; the quiet attunement of small creatures that live in dark places close to stone
1.environment: Any (monstrous spiders are found throughout Oreka -- Great Forests, underground cave-systems, Giant ruins, Vestri mines, Dómnathar tunnel-fringes, and any enclosed environment where webs can be anchored; specific subspecies favour specific environments)
1.description: The web is not visible until you are in it. The silk is the colour of shadow and it clings to everything -- armour, hair, skin -- with an adhesive strength that is entirely out of proportion to its apparent fragility. Something moves at the edge of your torchlight. It has too many legs and too many eyes and it is larger than you want it to be.

1.name: Troglodyte
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- shallow-cave; the resonance of limestone and mineral-earth near the surface, cool and damp, the Lord of Stone's domain at its most accessible and least imposing
1.environment: Shallow Caves (the upper cave-systems, rock-shelters, and limestone karst formations found across Oreka -- specifically the shallow cave-zones above Vestri mining-depth, where natural cave-systems provide shelter, moisture, and protection from surface weather)
1.organization: Clutch (2-5), squad (6-11 plus 1-2 monitors of 3 HD), or band (20-80 plus 20% noncombatants plus 3-13 monitors of 3 HD plus 1 chieftain of 3-6 HD)
1.description: The cave-mouth smells before you reach it. The stench is organic, chemical, and deliberately weaponised -- a musk so thick it coats the back of your throat and makes your eyes water at thirty feet. The creatures that emerge from the smell are reptilian: bipedal, grey-green, scaled, with flat faces and cold eyes that regard you with a hostility that is not personal but absolute. You are warm-blooded. You do not belong in their caves. The stench intensifies. They are doing that on purpose.

1.name: Kobold Scout (Sorcerer 1)
flags: kobold
hostile
caster
special_qualities: Spells known: 0th -- acid splash, ray of frost, resistance; 1st -- magic missile
1.environment: Gatefall Reach and Deepwater Marches (Dómnathar-linked underground sites, Silence Breach Zones, Ashcrown Warren)
1.description: A crest-scaled kobold with a focused, calculating stare. Dust-stained sleeves conceal the sigil-ink on her arms where she has drawn her spell foci.

0.name: Goblin
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- goblinoids exist outside the elemental framework
0.environment: Any (goblins are the most widespread non-Kin people in Oreka -- found in every region, in every terrain, in every conceivable ecological niche from Deepwood caves to desert ruins to mountain tunnels to steppe-margin camps)
0.organization: Gang (4-9), band (10-100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th-6th level), warband (10-24 with worg mounts), or settlement (40-400 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1-2 lieutenants of 4th-5th level plus 1 leader of 6th-8th level plus 10-24 worg mounts plus 2-4 dire wolves)
0.description: It is three feet tall, green-skinned, loud, and there are always more of them than you expected. The one crouching behind the rock has a knife. The one in the tree has a shortbow. The one you did not see has just stolen your waterskin. Goblins are not dangerous individually -- a single goblin against a single armed Kin is a mismatch that ends quickly. But goblins are never single, never alone, and never out of ideas.

0.name: Kobold
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- kobolds exist outside the elemental framework, but their reptilian physiology and deep-underground habitation give them a faint, false earth-resonance that reads as 'part of the stone'
0.environment: Underground and Dómnathar Tunnels (natural cave systems, mine-tunnels, and -- significantly -- the outer corridors of Dómnathar tunnel-networks, where kobold warrens serve as a buffer-zone between the Deceivers and the surface)
0.organization: Gang (4-9), band (10-100 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1 leader of 4th-6th level), warband (10-24 plus 2-4 dire weasels), or tribe (40-400 plus 100% noncombatants plus 1 sergeant of 3rd level per 20 adults plus 1-2 lieutenants of 4th-5th level plus 1 leader of 6th-8th level plus 10-24 dire weasels)
0.description: The tunnel is too small for you. That is the point -- everything in kobold territory is too small for you, too narrow, too low-ceilinged, too tight to swing a weapon. You are on your hands and knees in a passage two feet wide and eighteen inches tall, and somewhere ahead of you, in the dark, something is waiting with a crossbow loaded and a tripwire set and the patient, ancient hatred of a species that has survived everything by being smaller, faster, and more vindictive than anything that hun...

0.name: Kodama
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the deep, slow earth-resonance of a living tree's connection to the soil
0.environment: Great Forests (the interior of the oldest forests -- wherever a tree has stood long enough and grown deep enough to develop a kodama; never in young growth, plantations, or recently-cleared areas)
0.organization: Solitary (one per tree, always) or grove (3-20, one per tree in a kodama-grove)
0.description: The sound is not a sound. It is a vibration -- a resonance that comes from the tree itself, from the ring of wood inside the bark, from the sap moving through channels that have been open for five hundred years. If you press your ear to the trunk and listen with more than your ears, you hear it: a pulse, slow and patient, like a heartbeat that measures its time in seasons rather than seconds. The kodama is not in the tree. The kodama is the tree's awareness of itself.

4.name: Gray Ooze
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- oozes exist outside the elemental framework
4.environment: Underground (any subterranean environment -- natural caves, mine-tunnels, Giant-era passages, Dómnathar corridors; most common in wet underground environments where runoff collects)
4.description: The stone floor is wet. Not water-wet -- acid-wet, the kind of wet that dissolves boot-leather in seconds and eats through chainmail in minutes. The puddle on the floor is not a puddle. It is three inches deep, six feet across, and moving toward you with the purposeful slowness of something that has never needed to hurry because nothing it touches survives long enough to escape.

4.name: Griffon
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the griffon is sky-and-mountain, the Youngest Brother's wind beneath the Lord of Stone's weight
4.environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills and temperate highlands (rocky hills and upland meadows with clear sight-lines, nesting on cliff-faces and high ledges -- the transition zone between the Giant's Teeth mountains and the lower grasslands)
4.description: The shadow passes over you first -- wide, fast, and shaped wrong for a bird. You look up and see something impossible: a lion's body, golden and muscular, suspended beneath an eagle's wings that span thirty feet. The front half is pure raptor -- hooked beak, taloned forelegs, golden feathers merging seamlessly into tawny fur at the shoulders. It banks, catches the thermal rising from the hillside, and circles once, looking down at you with a golden eagle's eye that misses nothing.

4.name: Hakuturi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- the living forest; root-water and dark soil, growth expressed as patience
4.environment: Great Forests (the old-growth stands of the Kovaka territories -- the southern and island-fringe forests where ancient trees have stood for millennia undisturbed; never found in young growth, managed forest, or areas cleared within living memory)
4.description: The tree is old. Older than the Kovaka settlement at its base, older than the trail that leads to it, older than anything human memory can account for. You came to cut a branch -- a single branch, for a canoe-rib -- and now you cannot lift the axe. Not because something is stopping you. Because the tree is looking at you. The bark has rearranged itself into a face that was always there and always watching, and the face is not angry. It is patient. It is waiting for you to understand why you sho...

4.name: Mimic
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- mimics exist outside the elemental framework entirely; their physiology registers as an absence that is not Dómnathar void but simply nothing
4.environment: Giant Ruins (the interior rooms, corridors, and vaults of Giant-era ruin-complexes throughout the Infinite Desert -- any structure where there are objects to mimic and prey to attract; secondary populations in Vestri deep-mines and Dómnathar-adjacent tunnel-systems)
4.description: The chest is the only thing in the room that is not covered in dust. You notice this, but you notice it too late -- your hand is already on the lid, and the lid is already on your hand. The surface beneath your fingers is warm and wet, and the chest is opening in a direction that chests do not open, and the inside of the chest is full of teeth.

4.name: Otyugh
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberrant; otyughs register on Kin-sense as a faint, unpleasant signal that doesn't map to any elemental framework
4.environment: City Sewers and Dungeons (the sewage-systems, refuse-pits, and underground waste-channels of Eruskan cities, Vestri mining-camps, and any settlement large enough to generate concentrated organic waste)
4.description: The sewer smells exactly as bad as you expected, but the thing in the sewer smells worse. It occupies the junction where three tunnels meet -- a bulbous, three-legged mass of mottled brown-grey flesh, waist-deep in the effluent, with two long tentacles rising from its body like the antennae of something that evolved specifically to live in places no other creature would tolerate. Its third appendage -- a thick, eyestalk on a muscular neck -- swivels toward you with the calm, unsurprised attentio...

4.name: Shedim
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- liminal; shedim exist in the twilight between elemental signatures, in the perceptual gaps where Kin-sense transitions between readings and cannot resolve clearly
4.environment: Twilight Zones (the liminal spaces at the edges of goblin settlements -- the grey areas between day and night, between forest and clearing, between the wild static of goblin communities and the blank absence of uninhabited wilderness; shedim manifest where the world is between states)
4.description: The twilight is wrong. You have walked through twilight a hundred times -- the grey hour between day and night when the world is neither lit nor dark, when shadows have no source and distance becomes unreliable. This twilight does not end. It has been grey for twenty minutes, and the sun is not setting and has not risen, and the goblin settlement ahead is quiet in a way that goblin settlements are never quiet. Something stands in the gap between the last house and the treeline -- a shape that i...

4.name: Kobold Wizard (Wizard 4)
flags: kobold
hostile
caster
4.environment: Kinsweave Ruins (buried Deceiver artifact chambers in the Six Kings' ruins: Mytro, Kalite, Hylen, Thrush, Andrio, Pryee; Highridge lava-tubes); underground; also Gatefall, Deepwater
4.description: An elder kobold in a high-collared robe sewn with brass reading-lenses. She has catalogued and copied more Deceiver scrolls than any living Kin scholar; she simply does not share.

4.name: Hobgoblin Tunnel-Warden (Fighter 3 / Ranger 1)
flags: hobgoblin
hostile
special_qualities: Knows every turn and side-passage of his patrol circuit
4.environment: Gatefall Reach (Silence Breach tunnel networks and Dómnathar burrow-forts beneath the southeastern foothills); underground
4.organization: patrol (1 warden plus 4-6 hobgoblins and 2 war-wargs)
4.description: A veteran hobgoblin with a deep scar from jaw to temple and the gait of a man whose back has been broken and reset. He knows the tunnels better than any map.

4.name: Dark Dwarf Warrior (Fighter 3)
flags: dwarf
dark_dwarf
hostile
4.environment: Gatefall Reach, Deepwater Marches, Infinite Desert Glass Wastes, Eternal Steppe Burnt Hollows, and any active Dómnathar site; underground
4.description: A broad, scarred dwarf in iron plate darkened with lamp-black. The Deceivers' War left his clan underground; four generations later, this is what the clan has become.

11.name: Grootslang
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- primordial; the grootslang's affinity is not the refined, balanced expression of later elemental creatures but raw, undivided fire-and-earth from before the Elemental Lords distinguished between their domains
11.environment: Volcanic Caves (the deep volcanic tube-systems of the Scorchspires and Emberfang Mountains -- not surface calderas but the interior passages where magma flows beneath the mountain-skin; specifically the caves that Taraf-Imro traditions mark as sacred and forbidden)
11.description: The cave breathes. That is not metaphor -- the air moves in and out of the volcanic tube in a rhythm that predates language, and the rhythm is the breathing of the thing that lives inside. It has lived there since before the Giants came. Since before the Kin were shaped. Since before the Elemental Lords withdrew from the world and left their domains to spin on without them. It is the size of a building -- an elephant's body fused with a serpent's tail, scaled in obsidian, radiating heat that ma...

11.name: Lindworm
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- deep; the slow pressure of mountains, the heat of the world's bones beneath the crust
11.environment: Giant's Teeth Deep Shafts (the deepest mine-shafts, natural fissures, and geothermal vents below Vestri mining depth -- particularly the Undershafts beneath Stoneheart, the flooded deep-tunnels of Ironwell, and the volcanic fissures of the inner Giant's Teeth)
11.description: The shaft goes deeper than the Vestri mapped it. The stonework ended three levels up -- down here, the walls are raw mountain, the seams of ore glinting in your lantern-light like veins in a living body. The air is warm and wet and smells of acid. You hear it before you see it: a vast, slow scraping, like a ship's hull dragging across rock, and the sound of something breathing through a mouth full of venom. The lindworm fills the tunnel. Its body is serpentine and armoured in scales the colour...

11.name: Şahmeran
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- deep-tunnel ancient; the resonance of stone that has been stone since the world began, undisturbed, unmined, unknown -- earth so deep and so old that it predates even the Lord of Stone's awareness of it
11.environment: Deep Tunnels (the deepest tunnel-systems beneath the Giant's Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Kinsweave -- below Vestri mining-depth, below Dómnathar operational zones, in the primordial rock-chambers where nothing has walked since the world formed; şahmeran inhabit sealed chambers that they have occupied for millennia)
11.description: The tunnel opens into a chamber that the Vestri deep-shaft maps do not show -- unmarked, unmined, sealed behind a wall that someone built from the other side. The wall is old. The chamber beyond it is older. The air is warm and still and carries a scent that is not sulphur, not stone-dust, not the metallic tang of deep rock -- it is sweet, like honey left in the sun, and underneath the sweetness there is something else, something that your hindbrain recognises before your conscious mind does: v...

11.name: Zmey Gorynych
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- volcanic intelligence; the resonance of fire at its most calculating, the Lady's domain in its predatory, thinking aspect -- not the mindless burn of a wildfire or the nurturing warmth of a forge-bond but the deliberate, patient, strategic heat of magma that chooses where to flow
11.environment: Scorchspires (Rarozhki) -- the deep volcanic valleys, caldera-rims, and magma-lake caverns of the Scorchspires' inner range -- specifically the zones too hot for any creature without fire immunity, where the zmey gorynych lairs in comfort while everything else is kept at bay by temperature alone
11.description: Three heads. That is what the Rarozhki scouts reported, and three heads is what you see: three serpentine necks rising from a body built like a fortress of red-black scales, each head armoured, horned, and breathing independently. The left head watches you. The centre head watches the sky. The right head watches the tunnel behind it. Nothing approaches a zmey gorynych from a single direction, because the zmey gorynych is watching every direction simultaneously. Each mouth trails smoke. Each e...

11.name: Dómnathar Void-Construct
flags: construct
domnathar
hostile
boss
special_attacks: Silence aura (30 ft, DC 15 Will or silenced)
special_qualities: Construct traits (immune mind-affecting, fortification 50%, no Con)
Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
11.environment: Deepwater Spur Tower (command levels); Gatefall deep; Infinite Desert Fusion-Cell
11.description: A man-shaped husk of grey-veined obsidian, eight feet tall, built around a core of absolutely empty air where most creatures would have breath. It moves in total silence even when it runs.

14.name: Huallallo
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- primordial; like the grootslang, the huallallo's affinity is raw and undivided, from an age when fire and earth were not yet separate concepts
14.environment: Giant's Teeth Volcanoes (the active and semi-active volcanic peaks in the southern Giant's Teeth, where the mountain range meets the Scorchspires -- specifically the calderas and lava-tube systems that Orean mythology identifies as 'the Lady's Anger')
14.description: The mountain is angry. That is what the Orean herders say when the ground shakes and the sky turns orange and the air tastes of sulphur thick enough to chew. The mountain is angry, and the anger has a shape. It walks out of the caldera on legs of cooling basalt, trailing magma behind it like a cloak, and it is thirty feet tall and burning from the inside. Its eyes are white-hot. Its hands are fists of volcanic glass. The Orean do not run from it -- you cannot outrun a volcanic eruption. They p...

12.name: Indrik
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ancient; the indrik is younger than the grootslang and huallallo but still immensely old, a creature from the age when the Elemental Lords' domains were raw but beginning to differentiate
12.environment: Scorchspire Interior (the deep volcanic valleys and caldera-basins of the Scorchspire mountain range's interior -- terrain too hostile for permanent Kin habitation, accessible only through passes that the Rarozhki have sealed and marked as sacred)
12.description: The mountain is supposed to be empty. The Rarozhki sealed this valley three generations ago -- declared it sacred, marked the approaches, and turned back every expedition. You came anyway, because the things people seal away are usually valuable. Now you understand why they sealed it. The creature standing in the valley-floor is the size of a house -- a massive, four-legged beast with a single spiralling horn of black obsidian rising from its skull, its body armoured in volcanic stone plates th...

12.name: Roper
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- living stone; the resonance of mineral deposits that have developed awareness, stone that has been stone so long it has learned to hunger
12.environment: Deep Caverns (the deepest natural cave-systems of the Giant's Teeth, the Scorchspires, and the Ashen Crown -- well below Vestri mining-depth, in the eternal-dark zones where no light has ever reached and the only sounds are the drip of groundwater and the occasional groan of settling rock)
12.description: The stalagmite has an eye. You do not see it immediately -- you have been walking through the cavern for twenty minutes, and the stalagmites here are tall and thick and densely packed, and your attention is on the ground (uneven, slick, treacherous). But the Vestri miner leading you has stopped, and he is looking at a specific stalagmite with an expression that has gone very still, very controlled, and when you follow his gaze you see it: a single yellow eye, lidless and unblinking, set in the...

10.name: Supay
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- sovereign depth; the resonance of the deepest earth, the stone that has never seen light, the Lord of Stone's domain at its most absolute and most ancient -- not the worked-stone of mines or the living-stone of mountains but the primordial substrate beneath everything
10.environment: Deep Caverns (the deepest natural cave-systems beneath the Giant's Teeth foothills -- specifically the ancient mine-shafts and subterranean chambers that Orean mountain-communities have mapped and sealed, where the earth is old enough to have its own ruler)
10.organization: Solitary (always -- a supay claims a domain and rules it alone)
10.description: The mine-shaft is old -- older than the Vestri tunnels above it, older than the Orean settlements in the foothills, old enough that the tool-marks on the walls were made by hands larger than any Kin's. The air at the bottom of the shaft is not stale. It is warm, the wrong kind of warm -- not geothermal warmth but the warmth of a presence, of something breathing in the dark. The Orean miners who mapped this shaft went no further than this landing, and the reason is carved into the wall in Old Or...

10.name: Dómnathar Sorcerer (Sorcerer 8)
flags: domnathar
hostile
caster
kin_silent
boss
special_qualities: Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
Void Resonance (Su): on a confirmed hit with any spell, target must save or take 1 point of Wisdom drain as reality feels wrong.
10.environment: Deepwater Spur Tower; Gatefall deep tunnels
10.description: A Dómnathar caster whose spell-focus is a small blank glass sphere she carries on a silver chain. When she casts, the sphere fills with black smoke for a moment and then clears.
